question: Why did the conveyor stop?
most_likely_cause: Photoeye PE-101 appears blocked — the conveyor stopped on a photo-eye jam, not a drive or motor fault.
why: Photoeye PE-101 beam is blocked (DI_05 latched)
Ladder asserts the photo-eye jam (anomaly A12)
Motor RPM drops to 0 — but the GS10 reports NO drive fault
evidence_for: Anomaly A12 'Photo-eye jam' fired (di05_photoeye blocked ≥ threshold).
evidence_against: No GS10 fault code present → this is NOT a VFD fault (rules out the drive).

manuals_used.title: DURApulse GS10 User Manual (GS10M)
manuals_used.why: Confirm the GS10 is healthy (no drive fault, V/Hz, comm) so a stopped conveyor is NOT a VFD fault — points the diagnosis at the photoeye.

manuals_used.title: GS10 Manual Ch.06 — Fault & Warning Codes
manuals_used.why: Decode/rule out GS10 fault codes (E07, CE/CF comm). Absence of a fault code is the evidence-against a VFD cause.

manuals_used.title: Micro820 Programmable Controllers User Manual (2080-UM005)
manuals_used.why: Confirms DI_05 wiring + serial (8N2/9600) so the photoeye input (di05_photoeye) is read correctly.

manuals_used.title: Photoeye PE-101 notes (UNKNOWN_MODEL)
manuals_used.why: The cleaning/check procedure for the photoeye on DI_05 — the recommended technician actions for a blocked beam. Exact model unknown (honest).

manuals_used.title: Factory I/O documentation
manuals_used.why: The conveyor + diffuse/retro-reflective sensor behaviour in the visual layer.

manuals_used.title: GS10 + Micro820 Integration Guide (wiring + comm + tag map)
manuals_used.why: The authoritative wiring + register map: DI_05 photoeye, GS10 HRs, comm params. The IO map the diagnosis grounds tags against.

manuals_used.title: Modbus server / register map (MbSrvConf_ConvSimple_v1.9.xml — the deployed map; v2.1 reuses it)
manuals_used.why: Coils + holding registers actually deployed (di05_photoeye at coil 000023) — proves the photoeye tag is real and addressed.

manuals_used.title: Conv_Simple anomaly rules (A12 photo-eye jam)
manuals_used.why: Defines A12 'Photo-eye jam' (di05_photoeye blocked >= X s) — the deterministic rule that fires the scenario.

similar_history: Bench log: a 'flaky_photoeye' event was captured before (plc/conv_simple_anomaly/live_logger.py --label flaky_photoeye). Same DI_05 signature.
technician_checks: Clean the PE-101 lens (product debris / condensation / label adhesive).
Clear the beam and confirm DI_05 (di05_photoeye) toggles in the PLC.
Inspect the belt for a jammed item holding the beam broken.
Confirm the GS10 shows NO fault (no E07 / CE comm fault) — drive is healthy.
human_review: Confirm on the bench before acting — this is MIRA's most likely hypothesis.
PE-101 exact model is UNKNOWN_MODEL (not on file) — read the sensor part number off the bench and record it in demo/evidence/photoeye/notes.md.
